questions.question: Should multi-outcome scenarios (3+ outcomes) be tested, or only binary (yes/no) cases with probability values 0, 0.5, 1?
questions.why_it_matters: The component supports up to 8 color-coded outcomes and cycles colors. Testing only binary cases would miss color cycling and multi-segment layout behavior.

questions.question: Should edge cases like empty outcomes array or marginals with missing keys be tested?
questions.why_it_matters: The component uses `marginals[o.id] ?? 0` fallback. Testing this guard ensures robustness, but may be out of scope for the stated requirements.

questions.question: Should the title attribute on bar segments (tooltip text) be asserted, or only the visible percentage text in the labels?
questions.why_it_matters: The component renders probability info in two places: title attributes on bar segments and visible span labels. The task says 'displaying percentage text' which could mean either or both.

assumptions.assumption: Tests should use @testing-library/react with the existing vitest + jsdom setup (vitest.config.ts already configures jsdom, setup.ts imports jest-dom matchers).
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to set up an alternative test runner or environment.

assumptions.assumption: Binary outcomes (Yes/No) are sufficient for testing probability values 0, 0.5, and 1 as stated in the task description.
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need additional test cases for 3+ outcome markets to cover color cycling and multi-segment layout.

assumptions.assumption: Bar width styling can be asserted by checking the inline style `width` property on rendered div elements.
assumptions.if_wrong: May need to use getComputedStyle or a different approach to verify styling.

assumptions.assumption: Percentage text is the visible span text formatted by formatProbability (e.g., '50.0%'), and title attributes on bar segments should also be verified.
assumptions.if_wrong: Tests would need to be scoped differently to match only visible text or only tooltips.

assumptions.assumption: The test file should be placed at frontend/src/__tests__/ProbabilityBar.test.tsx as specified in the task.
assumptions.if_wrong: N/A - path is explicitly stated in the task description.
